channel.title: NYT > Business
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Honda Posts First Ever Annual Loss After Pullback From E.V.s
item.description: Honda posted its first loss since 1957 as it took a multibillion-dollar hit from scaling back its electric-vehicle plans.
item.dc:creator: River Akira Davis
item.media:description: A Honda dealership in Osaka, Japan.

item.title: Mamdani Urges State to Block Western Union’s Deal for Intermex
item.description: New York City’s mayor said the company’s proposed acquisition of Intermex could make it costlier for immigrants to send money abroad.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Hirsch
item.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani of New York sent a state agency a letter asking it to block Western Union’s proposed $500 million acquisition of Intermex.

item.title: Trump Administration Will Withhold $1.3 Billion in Medicaid Payments to California
item.description: Vice President JD Vance said the state had not done enough to fight fraud in the public health insurance program.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kliff
item.category: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance said that California had failed to combat fraud in Medicaid.

item.title: What to Watch at Trump and Xi’s U.S.-China Summit
item.description: Here’s what to know about the biggest sources of tension in U.S.-China relations before the first summit in Beijing in nine years between the nations’ leaders.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport, Ana Swanson and Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump arrived in Beijing on Wednesday.

item.title: Senate Confirms Kevin Warsh as Fed Chair
item.description: President Trump’s pick to replace Jerome H. Powell takes the helm at a highly uncertain moment for the economy and with heightened concern about the Federal Reserve’s ability to operate independently.
item.dc:creator: Colby Smith
item.media:description: Kevin M. Warsh has vowed to embark on “regime change” at the Federal Reserve.

item.title: Eric Trump, Who Runs the Family Business, Hops Along on the Plane to China
item.description: The president’s middle son is said to be joining his father in a “personal capacity” rather than to make business deals.
item.dc:creator: Ben Protess
item.media:description: President Trump and his delegation, including his son Eric Trump, at the Beijing Capital Airport on Wednesday.

item.title: Trump Administration Pushes I.R.S. to Identify Undocumented Immigrants
item.description: The deliberations are a sign that the Trump administration may once again try to harness the power of the I.R.S. to advance its immigration agenda.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Duehren and Zolan Kanno-Youngs
item.media:credit: Mark Abramson for The New York Times
item.media:description: A tax preparation clinic in Los Angeles. The Internal Revenue Service has in effect looked the other way on immigration status, focusing instead on encouraging people to pay the taxes that they may owe.

item.title: An Uncertain World Awaits the Reopening of the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Fatih Birol of the International Energy Agency, who orchestrated a multinational release of oil reserves, detailed the risks facing the economy now and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Eshe Nelson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Dmitry Kostyukov for The New York Times
item.media:description: “The single most important solution to this problem today is fully and unconditionally opening up of the strait,” Fatih Birol, who leads the International Energy Agency, said in an interview this week.

item.title: Rich Danker, RFK Jr.’s Top Spokesman, Resigns in Protest Over New Vaping Policy
item.description: In a letter to President Trump, the spokesman, Rich Danker, said allowing the sale of flavored e-cigarettes would enhance their appeal to children.
item.dc:creator: Sheryl Gay Stolberg
item.media:credit: Michael A. McCoy for The New York Times
item.media:description: The resignation comes at a fraught time for the Department of Health and Human Services, which now has three major vacancies.

item.title: Why the Bombing of Iran Tied the U.S. More Closely to China
item.description: As the U.S. tries to rebuild its weapons stockpiles drained in the Iran war, it will need access to rare-earth minerals, an industry China dominates.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson and John Ismay
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A destroyed building in Tehran last month. The war in Iran is expected to figure into talks between the United States and China in Beijing this week.

item.title: A Start-Up Aiming to Make Geothermal Energy Mainstream Goes Public
item.description: Fervo Energy, which uses drilling techniques from the oil and gas industry to produce power from the earth’s heat, raised $1.9 billion in an initial public offering.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Penn and Brad Plumer
item.media:credit: Brandon Thibodeaux for The New York Times
item.media:description: A Fervo Energy project near Milford, Utah. The company is trying to adapt drilling techniques from the oil and gas industry to unlock vast amounts of energy from the earth’s interior.

item.title: Dozens of Polymarket Bets Show Signs of Insider Trading, The Times Finds
item.description: Dozens of long-shot bets on Polymarket, from the war with Iran to the cryptocurrency market, have defied the odds, according to a New York Times examination.
item.dc:creator: Stuart A. Thompson and David Yaffe-Bellany

item.title: Fuel Prices Drive Sales of E.V.s, Just Not in the U.S.
item.description: Electric vehicle sales have soared in Europe and much of the rest of the world, but Americans are still hesitant.
item.dc:creator: Jack Ewing
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:description: High fuel prices are weighing on sales of vehicles that run on gasoline or diesel.

item.title: With a Friend in Trump, the Tobacco Industry Secures a Lucrative Win
item.description: In a dispute over vapes, the president sided with tobacco companies that filled his groups’ coffers over his own F.D.A. commissioner, who resigned in protest.
item.dc:creator: Christina Jewett and Kenneth P. Vogel
item.media:description: The F.D.A. issued new guidance that could pave the way for major tobacco companies to begin selling flavored vapes less than a week after industry executives met with President Trump.

item.title: Wholesale Prices Jumped in April, in Latest Sign of War’s Economic Ripples
item.description: The Producer Price Index rose in April at its fastest pace in four years, government data showed, a day after consumer prices showed inflation was surging.
item.dc:creator: Ben Casselman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Tariff Refunds Begin to Reach Businesses as Trump Lashes Out at Court
item.description: The government must return about $160 billion, plus interest, collected from duties deemed illegal and potentially more if it loses a related tariff case.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm
item.media:description: The tariff refunds come at a difficult political moment for President Trump, who is visiting China this week for a series of high-stakes trade talks.

item.title: Why a Warsh-Led Fed May Keep Interest Rates Higher for Longer
item.description: Kevin Warsh, President Trump’s pick to run the Federal Reserve, would face a growing inflation challenge if he’s confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Ross Sorkin, Bernhard Warner, Sarah Kessler, Michael J. de la Merced, Niko Gallogly and Brian O’Keefe
item.media:description: Kevin Warsh, President Trump’s pick to run the Fed, appears set to inherit a central bank at a crossroads as inflation surges.

item.title: What to Know About the Federal Gas Tax Trump Wants to Suspend
item.description: President Trump said he would like to suspend the 18.4-cent-a-gallon tax, but it’s a move that may save drivers only a few dollars a month.
item.dc:creator: Emmett Lindner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Gas prices have climbed more than 50 percent since February.

item.title: Can Three Auction Houses Sell $2.6 Billion Worth of Art in One Week?
item.description: Five luxury artworks hold the key to the spring season, one of the most anticipated sales in years. Major buyers are looking past female and younger artists and toward tradition.
item.dc:creator: Tim F. Schneider, Julia Halperin and Zachary Small

item.title: Tired of Hacked Passwords? Help Is on the Way.
item.description: Apps from Apple, Google and others can assist in making your online accounts more secure, even as new ways of logging in continue to take off.
item.dc:creator: J. D. Biersdorfer
item.media:description: A password manager, like the 1Password app shown here, serves as a secure vault for all your user names, passwords and other private information.

item.title: Trump’s Shrinking Ambitions on China
item.description: The president came into office planning harsher trade moves on China than on the rest of the world. Here’s why he’s had to scale them back.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson
item.media:description: When President Trump tried to force China into making concessions last year by threatening extreme tariffs, the tactic backfired, forcing the United States to pare back its goals.

item.title: How China Could Wield Its Control of Rare Earths Against Trump
item.description: A central question hanging over the summit this week is whether China will agree to extend a temporary postponement of even tougher rare-earth export controls.
item.dc:creator: Keith Bradsher
item.media:description: Steel mills, chemical factories and rare-earth refineries in Baotou, China. The Trump administration said it was in frequent communication with China about rare earths.

item.title: Here’s How Trump’s Trade War on China Ended Up in a Stalemate
item.description: As President Trump prepares to meet with Xi Jinping, a trade war that once threatened to freeze commerce between the two countries has given way to an uneasy truce.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Stevenson
item.media:credit: Qilai Shen for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump imposed tariffs on Chinese goods far steeper than those on any other country.

item.title: Justice Dept. Officials Consider Settling Trump Suit Against I.R.S.
item.description: One of the settlement terms under review is for the I.R.S. to drop any audits of the president, his family members and businesses.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Duehren and Alan Feuer
item.media:description: President Trump sued the Internal Revenue Service in January over the leak of his tax returns during his first term.

item.title: Conan O’Brien Will Return as Oscars Host
item.description: It will be the first time anyone has done the job for three straight years since Billy Crystal in the early 1990s.
item.dc:creator: John Koblin
item.category: Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences
item.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
item.media:description: Conan O’Brien hosting the Academy Awards in March. He has taken to the job with gusto, and has been widely praised.

item.title: Marty Makary, Trump’s F.D.A. Commissioner, Resigns After Weeks of Pressure
item.description: The agency’s top food official will step in to the role after Dr. Makary privately said he opposed the administration decision to approve flavored vapes.
item.dc:creator: Christina Jewett
item.media:credit: Al Drago for The New York Times
item.media:description: Dr. Marty Makary at times tried to advance his own principles alongside the administration’s goals, which at times were at odds with the powerful food, tobacco and pharmaceutical industries.

item.title: EBay, the Old-School E-Commerce Site, Finds Its Place in Modern Retail
item.description: The online marketplace has remade itself by focusing on collectibles and other high-end goods — and attracted an unwelcome takeover bid from GameStop.
item.dc:creator: Kim Bhasin
item.media:description: Sales are up at eBay, profits have improved, and its stock price has doubled over the past two years.

item.title: Joni Lamb, Whose Christian TV Station Went Global, Dies at 65
item.description: She and her husband, Marcus Lamb, founded Daystar Television Network, which reached more than 200 countries and made the couple into televangelism stars.
item.dc:creator: Alex Traub
item.media:credit: Lawrence Jenkins/The Dallas Morning News, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Joni Lamb in 2001. She was Daystar’s leading talk show host, moderating shows like “Taking a Break With Joni” and “Joni Table Talk.”

item.title: What to know about the report.
item.dc:creator: Lydia DePillis

item.title: UK Bond Yields Rise With Starmer’s Future as Prime Minister in Doubt
item.description: The yields on British debt pushed higher as the country’s prime minister, Keir Starmer, confronted a rebellion from members of his party.
item.dc:creator: Eshe Nelson
item.media:credit: Emli Bendixen for The New York Times
item.media:description: Investors are betting that the central bank will raise interest rates instead of cutting them this year, in turn raising borrowing costs for mortgage holders.

item.title: Tomato Prices Soar as War, Tariffs and Weather Affect a Popular Crop
item.description: Tomato prices surged nearly 40 percent in April, the Consumer Price Index showed.
item.dc:creator: Julie Creswell
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Tomatoes were the culprit behind a rise in food prices at grocery stores in April.

item.title: CPI Shows Inflation Accelerating to 3.8% Annually in April After Weeks of War in Iran
item.description: The Consumer Price Index rose 3.8% in April from a year earlier as higher energy costs replaced tariffs as the driver of higher prices for Americans.
item.dc:creator: Lydia DePillis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: How Much Power Did Microsoft Have Over OpenAI?
item.description: The technology giant’s chief executive faced a grilling in court over whether it exerted too much control over what was meant to be an A.I. nonprofit.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Ross Sorkin, Bernhard Warner, Sarah Kessler, Michael J. de la Merced, Niko Gallogly, Brian O’Keefe and Grady McGregor
item.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
item.media:description: Satya Nadella, Microsoft’s C.E.O., took the stand yesterday in the trial that’s riveting the tech world: Elon Musk v. OpenAI.

item.title: The Iran War Is Taking the Color Out of Japan’s Best-Known Snack Bags
item.description: The food giant Calbee said shortages of naphtha, a crude-oil derivative used in inks, were forcing it to switch to black-and-white packaging for its salty products.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández and Kiuko Notoya

item.title: EBay Rejects GameStop’s $55 Billion Takeover Bid
item.description: The online marketplace called the cash-and-stock proposal “neither credible nor attractive.”
item.dc:creator: Lauren Hirsch
item.media:description: The headquarters of eBay in San Jose, Calif. GameStop announced a proposal last week to combine with the company, which is nearly four times its size.

item.title: China Sought Access to Anthropic’s Newest A.I. The Answer Was No.
item.description: The latest artificial intelligence models from Anthropic and OpenAI are extending the United States’ lead over China and intensifying the rivalry between the countries.
item.dc:creator: Dustin Volz, Julian E. Barnes, Sheera Frenkel and Tripp Mickle
item.media:credit: Marissa Leshnov for The New York Times
item.media:description: A growing number of national security officials and analysts have begun to liken the intensifying competition between China and the United States over artificial intelligence to the Cold War’s nuclear arms race.

item.title: YouTube Plays Matchmaker for Sponsors and Stars
item.description: The company is trying to help streaming influencers, who are increasingly being wooed by competitors like Netflix and TikTok.
item.dc:creator: John Koblin
item.media:credit: Brian Karlsson for The New York Times
item.media:description: Kareem Rahma, the host of the popular Instagram and TikTok series “Subway Takes.”

item.title: Inflation Jumps as War-Driven Energy Costs Drive Up Prices
item.description: President Trump has declared the cease-fire on “life support,” and investors are taking a cautious approach as the economic effects of the war become more apparent.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vessels in the Strait of Hormuz on Friday. Iran has effectively shut down the narrow waterway.

item.title: How an ‘Impossible’ Idea Led to a Pancreatic Cancer Breakthrough
item.description: The new strategy also holds promise for lung and colon tumors. Here’s how scientists discovered it.
item.dc:creator: Gina Kolata and Rebecca Robbins
item.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times

item.title: Steel Tariffs Are Raising the Price of Canned Foods
item.description: The cost to put food in cans remains high because manufacturers must still import steel from overseas. U.S. Steel plans to reopen a tin-plate factory.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis and Ross Mantle
item.media:description: Rolls of tin-plated steel at Ohio Coatings in Yorkville, Ohio.

item.title: Why Your Life Is Now on Subscriptions
item.description: As companies look to build cash flow and loyalty, everything from heated car seats to earthworm deliveries can become a recurring charge on your credit card.
item.dc:creator: Sopan Deb
item.media:description: From streaming services to car features, more companies are moving toward a model that turns customers into lifelong subscribers.

item.title: How Do Two London Luxury Hotels Compare? Testing the Chancery Rosewood and Raffles at the OWO.
item.description: Raffles at the OWO and the Chancery Rosewood recently opened in landmarks once teeming with generals, diplomats and spies. Both promise pampering and a dash of history. Do they deliver?
item.dc:creator: Amy Virshup
item.media:description: The stairway at Raffles at the OWO is made of marble and alabaster and was designed to communicate power when the building served as a military headquarters.

item.title: China Seeks A.I. Independence, Weakening Trump’s Leverage
item.description: Before this week’s U.S.-Chinese summit, Beijing reached a milestone in its quest for technological self-sufficiency.
item.dc:creator: Meaghan Tobin
item.media:credit: Kelsey McClellan for The New York Times
item.media:description: DeepSeek said for the first time that its new model had been optimized to run on chips made by the Chinese tech giant Huawei.

item.title: China Increasingly Views Trump’s America as an Empire in Decline
item.description: For decades, many Chinese viewed the United States with a mix of admiration, envy and resentment. President Trump’s volatile second term shattered that image.
item.dc:creator: Li Yuan

item.title: Byron Allen Is Buying a Controlling Stake in BuzzFeed
item.description: Jonah Peretti, BuzzFeed’s co-founder 20 years ago, will step down as chief executive and serve as its president of A.I.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Mullin
item.media:credit: Bethany Mollenkof for The New York Times
item.media:description: A company associated with Byron Allen, shown in 2019, will pay $120 million for a 52 percent stake in BuzzFeed.

item.title: As Trump Dismisses Iran Truce Plan, World Braces for Lasting Economic Pain
item.description: President Trump said the fragile cease-fire was on “life support,” and India’s leader urged residents to cut back on purchases and travel.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Sanam Mahoozi, Michael Levenson and Max Bearak
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Tehran last week. Iranian officials are facing domestic pressure to reach a deal as the country’s economic problems have grown only more dire since the war.

item.title: Dean Buntrock, Maestro of Waste Management, Dies at 94
item.description: Anticipating how the environmental movement would affect trash disposal in the United States, he built a 12-truck garbage company into a coast-to-coast behemoth.
item.dc:creator: Trip Gabriel
item.media:description: Dean Buntrock in 1984. He foresaw that an increase in environmental regulation would require a more capital-intensive garbage industry.

item.title: Trump Proposes Suspending Federal Gas Tax Until Prices Fall
item.description: Congress would have to agree to any suspension, and dropping federal taxes would not make up for the steep increase in prices since the war began.
item.dc:creator: Shawn McCreesh
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Gas prices have risen about 50 percent since the United States and Israel began the war with Iran on Feb. 28.

item.title: RFK Jr. Is Driving a Vast Inquiry Into Vaccines, Despite His Public Silence
item.description: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. has toned down his public criticism of vaccines, under orders from the White House. But inside his department, a sprawling research effort is a top priority.
item.dc:creator: Christina Jewett and Sheryl Gay Stolberg
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
